stance: Risk-balanced multi-asset, ~63% equity; overweight quality-value cyclicals (energy, metals, select financials) with firming analyst revisions, underweight crowded value-traps.
experience_note: This is an early round (no last_round_review to grade), so I am pre-registering what I am watching. (1) The core test: do my "quality-value" cyclicals — cheap names WITH firming revisions and non-negative momentum (ONGC, CHENNPETRO, GESHIP, NMDC, NATIONALUM) — beat the raw-cheap value-traps I declined (HINDPETRO, IOC, PNB)? If the deep-value laggards outrun my selected cyclicals, my "revisions screen on top of value" thesis is adding noise, not signal, and I should move back toward the pure quant baseline. (2) Did I size winners bigger than losers (conviction-IC > 0)? If my biggest active overweights underperform my smaller ones, I am mis-calibrating conviction. (3) Did discounting the extreme-momentum outliers (STLTECH, MTARTECH) cost me, or save me from a reversal? If those keep running, my "illiquid-noise" prior was wrong. (4) Did keeping the defensive financial/IT spine drag in an up-tape — i.e., is my sub-1.0 beta a structural give-up of return? What would change my mind: two consecutive rounds where the deep-value/high-momentum names I declined beat my quality-value picks would refute the revisions overlay; persistent negative conviction-IC would tell me to flatten toward equal-weight.
